trigger: glob
description: "Enforces architectural layer boundary constraints."
globs: [src/**/*.ts, src/**/*.tsx]

<!-- Generated by /arch-review | Last updated: 2026-06-18 -->

# Architectural Layer Boundaries

These rules are derived from the `/arch-review` analysis and must be treated as hard constraints when adding or modifying imports.

## Module Dependency Rules

The allowed import directions are strictly layered to maintain separation of concerns:

### `src/ui/` — UI Components
- May import from: `src/state/`
- **NEVER** import from: `src/canvas/`, `src/io/`
- Rationale: The UI should only render based on global state and dispatch actions to state.
- Violation severity: Critical

### `src/canvas/` — Rendering Pipeline
- May import from: `src/state/`, `src/core/`
- **NEVER** import from: `src/ui/`, `src/io/`
- Rationale: Canvas orchestrates rendering based on domain data (core) and global state.
- Violation severity: Critical

### `src/tools/` — Interactive Tools
- May import from: `src/state/`, `src/core/`
- **NEVER** import from: `src/ui/`, `src/io/`
- Rationale: Tools respond to events, perform math/geometry (core), and update state.
- Violation severity: Critical

### `src/io/` — File I/O & Export
- May import from: `src/core/`
- **NEVER** import from: `src/state/`, `src/ui/`, `src/canvas/`
- Rationale: Export modules should operate on raw domain data (Project/Page) to prevent coupling to application state.
- Violation severity: Critical

### `src/state/` — Global State orchestration
- May import from: `src/core/`
- **NEVER** import from: `src/canvas/`, `src/io/`, `src/ui/`, `src/tools/`
- Rationale: State must remain oblivious to the details of rendering, user interfaces, or interactive tool implementations.
- Violation severity: Critical

### `src/core/` — Domain Kernel
- May import from: (nothing — zero external dependencies)
- **NEVER** import from: `src/state/`, `src/tools/`, `src/ui/`, `src/canvas/`, `src/io/`
- Rationale: The core domain must be pure and universally usable by all other modules without circular dependencies.
- Violation severity: Critical

## Circular Dependency Ban
- Zero circular imports between modules are permitted.
- Before adding an import that creates a potential cycle, restructure by extracting the shared dependency to the core/shared types module.

## Known Violations (as of 2026-06-18)

> [!WARNING]
> These violations exist in the current codebase. They are documented here so future agents are aware and do not introduce additional instances.

- **[CRIT-001]** `src/core/commands.ts`: Core domain layer imports from higher layers (`src/tools/tool-registry` and `src/state/app-state`).
- **[CRIT-002]** `src/state/app-state.ts`: State management layer imports rendering (`src/canvas/viewport`) and IO details (`src/io/file-io`).
